setting.note: Schlink never names the city, but its river, old bridge and hillside castle are unmistakably his own Heidelberg, on the Neckar in the Rhine-Neckar region. A fifteen-year-old and an older woman meet here; a generation later the story reaches out to a war-crimes court near Frankfurt and a former camp in the Alsace hills.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Bernhard Schlink, first published 1995 as Der Vorleser. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Reader, Bernhard Schlink (1995). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The slate of the Neckar at dusk and the grey weight the book carries. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
